title: Military equipment in 2015
wikitext:
[]: Military equipment in 2015             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Nov 03, 2007 11:09 am Apart from the unusual position of the UN and the rather ambiguous position of the JSSDF, isn't the military equipment seen rather unusual? I'm not just talking about troops here, I'm talking real BIG. Like, positron rifles, N2 mines, and those big missiles we see about. While a hoverjet would classify as a replacement for a helicopter, albeit an inefficient and illogical one, what would the UN do with a monstrosity like the positron rifle? The N2 mine is understandable, but what's the use of a precision weapon that powerful? Is it practical at all? And seriously, positrons? All very cool to shoot right through Ramiel's super A.T. Field but does anyone have any idea how hard it is to obtain such particles, or not have them smack straight into normal matter and turn into energy?

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sat Nov 03, 2007 11:30 am The positron rifle's origin is probably just a plot device to save the producers the trouble of explaining where it came from, unless one assumes that the SSDF had been infected by the American penchant for wasting lots of money on useless toys and intended to shoot down satellites with it. The N^2 bombs, on the other hand, are merely a manifestation of the Japanese cultural taboo against depictions of nuclear weapons.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Nov 03, 2007 11:40 am 1-the positron rifle is a plot device. Possibly it could be used against fixed enemy emplacements or incoming missiles or something, but that's just speculation. As I see it, it's just a prototype; they would build a scaled-down version later. 2- N2 bombs are not a way of getting around Japanese taboo around nukes; the mushroom cloud explosions are obvious vision parallels. The N2 Bombs struck me as really "Practical" actually, well rather, they don't strike me as "odd" ...they're a logical advancement of nuclear bomb technology, as well as a bit of a plot device that they don't have to worry about fallout. It basically sounds like a neutron bomb of today, with upgrades.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Nov 03, 2007 11:45 am Positrons are usually made by colliding accelerated electrons onto a large piece of tungsten, then they're diverted by magents into a linac where they're accelerated into a beam. However, as soon as the positrons leave the gun, they react with the air and you'd get a huge explosion in your face. Unless they can somehow create a vacuum along the path of the beam, this would never work at the scale that you see in the show. Meanwhile, in the real world: http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2007/10/071024090816.htm

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sat Nov 03, 2007 11:47 am V wrote: 2- N2 bombs are not a way of getting around Japanese taboo around nukes; the mushroom cloud explosions are obvious vision parallels.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Nov 03, 2007 11:52 am I think the key point is that they don't produce fallout. I mean "it's a bomb that can destroy a city, putting the power of god in the hands of man"....thematically doesn't really change. Maybe its "culturally significant" point is that Japan has a thing about fallout. I don't know, I'm actually not too knowledgeable about that. But the point is just "N2 Bombs seem like a logical extension of current weapons", while the positron rifle is "kind of far out there"

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Nov 03, 2007 11:55 am Not to mention that Positrons would convert into Gamma Rays, which are intensely radioactive. Who needs such a terrible weapon? A regular HE bomb can form a mushroom cloud. Heck, I've seen ordinary fireworks that form convincing mushroom clouds!

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Nov 03, 2007 12:25 pm A mushroom cloud is no more than smoke entrained in a spherical vortex -- a smoke ring, where the ring grows fat enough to fill the spherical envelope of the rotating motion.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Nov 03, 2007 12:41 pm UrsusArctos wrote: Not to mention that Positrons would convert into Gamma Rays, which are intensely radioactive. Who needs such a terrible weapon?

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sat Nov 10, 2007 6:39 pm I will also laugh... who will have positron rifles, which are so terrible? ME, (and my lackeys) back on topic, I am kind of queasy about how they could produce such things just 14 years after the second impact. They should like go back in technologie in some respect, but they produce this...almighty objects.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Nov 18, 2007 9:51 am The technology in NGE is pretty weird isn't it, you have loads of outdated equipment alongside super-futuristic stuff. Seele are the ones controlling the UN, and Seele knows about the Angel threat - so Seele would direct the UN down the path of weapons development for use against Angels (like the positron rifle), the people who developed the weapons like the positron rifle probably had no clue what the thing was going to be used for*, but the money was there so who would complain? *tho' the positron rifle reminds me of those huge lasers proposed in the 70s/80s (?) as part of the SDI shield, to shoot down nuclear missiles. Maybe that's what Seele told the UN it's development would be for. OR They could have said it was to stop "high-speed comets" from hitting the Earth, to stop future Impacts.

[]:             Quote Post by bap_120790 » Sun Nov 18, 2007 10:23 am So what do you think is the motif of having mega advanced weapons alongside seemingly obsolete weapons for their time?

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Nov 18, 2007 10:40 am One way of looking at it would be that Project E is gobbling up all the dosh, leaving the rest of the military (and a few third world nations, according to Seele) with no money whatsoever, which is why companies like Japan Heavy Chemical Industries had projects like Jet Alone - to undercut Nerv's funding (even though Nerv/Gehirn itself is under funded, they don‘t even have a good coffee machine!). I think it was Gendo in ep#21 who said all the resources of mankind had been piled into Tokyo-3 and the GeoFront.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Nov 18, 2007 11:32 am Zugzwang wrote: The technology in NGE is pretty weird isn't it, you have loads of outdated equipment alongside super-futuristic stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Nov 18, 2007 3:44 pm Its like the German Army's Blitzkrieg was supported by mule/horse driven supply lines. (No wonder they lost in the end in the Eastern Front)

[]:             Quote Post by Rommel » Sun Nov 18, 2007 5:08 pm N2 mines are essentially a more practical approach to warfare than nukes. A nuke is tactically, and some would say even strategically useless. Which is why the US has been developing MOABs and thermobaric weapons. N2 bombs being conventional explosives would be a good fallout free way of getting lots of bang for your buck.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Nov 18, 2007 5:13 pm Yes, but 14/15 years (or less) after a slight set back, and you can make that?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Nov 18, 2007 5:28 pm the technology of NGE is a bell-curve; they've got hoverjet-VTOL gunships and Evas but they still have things like tanks and beer cans and stuff. The example I use is of the holographic projection technology in the series; the Human Instrumentality Committee meetings are conducted by holovideo conference and from the end of Episode 08, Evas are fitted with holographic phones too (outside the entry plug), but it's a very "high end" technology only in use in really, REALLY advanced military stuff, while most just use normal phones and such.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Nov 18, 2007 5:34 pm Interesting. But it is still strange on the sudden technological push…

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Nov 18, 2007 5:45 pm well, after 2I, mankind was forced to Adapt so they needed tools to protect themselves. so they focused on military weapons and Evangelion while everything else hung still in evolution

I'm not just talking about troops here, I'm talking real BIG. Like, positron rifles, N2 mines, and those big missiles we see about. While a hoverjet would classify as a replacement for a helicopt
